name: Potato White Bread
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 cups unbleached white flour or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub the potatoes, slice them, and boil in water to cover until soft.
2. Place the potatoes and cooking water in a blender container. Add more water to bring the measure to 3 cups; puree.
3. Transfer to a large bowl. Stir in brown sugar and cool to lukewarm.
4. Sprinkle yeast over, stir to dissolve, and let rest at room temperature for 45 minutes.
5. Stir in 1 1/2 cups of flour and beat vigorously about 3 minutes until air bubbles form.
6. Stir in butter and salt; beat well and gradually add another 1 1/2 cups of the remaining flour.
7. Let rest at room temperature for 45 minutes.
8. Mound the remaining flour on a board. Turn dough out and, using a steel baker's scraper, knead in the flour until the dough is stiff enough to knead by hand. Add additional flour as needed to prevent sticking; knead 3 to 5 minutes.
9. Dough will be soft and slightly sticky. Divide into 2 equal portions. Knead briefly, form into 2 loaves, and place in oiled loaf pans.
10. Cover with a tea towel and let rise in a warm place for 45 minutes or until almost double in size.
11. Place in the oven; turn heat to 350°F and bake for 45 minutes or until bread tests done.
12. Turn out onto a wire rack; turn right side up and cool.
